'''Bernard John Dwyer''' (born 20 April 1967) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s. He played in the [[Super League]] and featured as a {{rlp|sr}}. He played for the [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] during a successful rugby league career - he enjoyed the distinction of winning every club honour in the English game.

Dwyer main strengths were viewed as his professional conduct and work rate. His commitment was regarded by observers as exemplary, and this was reflected in his testimonial year at St. Helens where 'the perpetual motion man' was recognised for his work rate. He retired from the sport in 2000.<ref name=TA26may2000>{{cite web |title=Injured Dwyer to quit |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/8055061.injured-dwyer-to-quit/ |website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |access-date=10 June 2022 |date=26 May 2000}}</ref>

Bernard Dwyer played {{rlp|hk}}, and scored 2-[[Try (rugby)|conversion]]s in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens']] 4-5 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1992 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]] on Sunday 18 October 1992.<ref name="1992-1993 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2745|title=1992–1993 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180713170348/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2745|archive-date=13 July 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 1995, Dwyer was part of the package that brought [[Paul Newlove]] to St. Helens from Bradford Bulls. The deal saw Bradford Bulls acquire three St. Helens players as well as a quarter of a million pounds.<ref name=TA26may2000/>

Dwyer played for [[Bradford Bulls]] at hooker in their [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|access-date=3 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|archive-date=8 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Dwyer played for [[Bradford Bulls]] at {{rlp|sr}} in the [[1999 Super League Grand Final]] which was lost to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].

Bernard is married to wife Jackie and has two children, the oldest Kelly lives in Sydney, whilst the youngest Connor plays Rugby League for Widnes.
